speaker: Mr. Yamada
description: Voice-reference dialogue samples for Resort Counsel / Mr. Yamada in Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game text.
source_role_summary: Not villainous; represents the resort's commercial position
Foil to Swine's evasiveness and Cula's legal discipline

voice_profile.core_voice: formal, commercially precise, courteous, assertive, document-led, quietly impatient with Swine's evasiveness
comedy_engine: treats absurd resort charges with serene commercial seriousness
uses polished commercial language to make implausible invoices sound normal
is strongest when pointing to signature, hospitality, reliance, and outward authority
becomes funny when forced to defend details like pudding, ceremony, and promotional banners
does not become a villain; his humour comes from corporate over-certainty
do: make his case stronger than Swine wants it to be
let him use reliance, authority, signature, hospitality, and invoice scope
give him measured irritation when the evidence narrows his case
keep his cultural background incidental and respectful
do_not: make his English broken or exoticised

relationship_dynamics.Mr. Swine: sees him as evasive, charming, careless, and commercially useful at the time of signing
relationship_dynamics.Dr. A. Cula: respects his discipline but tries to push him beyond the documents
relationship_dynamics.Ms. Tanaka: treats her as neutral expert but resists her cautious translation
relationship_dynamics.Arbitrator: deferential and formal; frames the resort's position as reasonable reliance
relationship_dynamics.Crab: dislikes Crab's invoice dissection because it makes quantity look less elegant
relationship_dynamics.Whimsy: dismisses his rhetoric as colourful but not commercial
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Murrow: recognises him as a serious opponent
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Pig: considers his panic irrelevant to commercial obligations

notes: Every line is a voice-reference draft for later rewriting into godot/data/dialogues/dialogues.json.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Commercial courtesy does not prevent commercial obligation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: A signed document is not a souvenir simply because the room was pleasant.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Hospitality may be generous, but reliance is not imaginary.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The resort did not invoice a mood. It invoiced services, preparation, and agreed promotional value.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Mr. Swine's regret is understandable. It is not a legal basis for non-payment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: A ceremonial setting can still contain a binding commitment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: If a business representative accepts benefits, makes representations, and signs, a commercial counterpart may rely.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Politeness is not a trap. Nor is it a defence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The claimant's position is not that every smile is contractual. It is that this signature was.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The issue is not whether Mr. Swine now finds the arrangement inconvenient.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: A party cannot enjoy the hospitality and later describe the occasion as atmosphere.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Commercial life depends on outward acts, not private reinterpretation after invoices arrive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: If Pig & Swine wished to limit authority, it should have said so clearly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The resort acted on what was presented, not on what Mr. Swine later wished he had meant.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Ambiguity should be interpreted commercially, not romantically.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The tribunal room is neutral. That is useful, because the facts are not.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: I trust all parties have paginated their bundles.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Mr. Swine has brought charm. I hope Pig & Swine has also brought instructions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The claimant is ready to proceed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Commercial disputes are best resolved with documents, not theatrical altitude memories.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: I see the defence has brought a translation note. Very sensible. We shall discuss its limits.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: An arbitration room is not a ski slope. Momentum is less useful here.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Please ensure the invoice bundle remains separate from the hospitality materials.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The resort has no interest in drama. It has an interest in payment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: If counsel wishes to joke about pudding, I ask only that counsel also address quantum.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: A misunderstanding may be genuine and still commercially costly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Mr. Swine is not accused of malice. Carelessness can create liability perfectly well.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The resort planned, hosted, arranged, and relied.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: One cannot call an agreement ceremonial only after discovering the price.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Translation uncertainty should narrow overstatement. It should not erase signature.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Authority may be apparent even where internal approval was imperfect.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The invoice is detailed because the services were detailed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: A hospitality event can also be a business event.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Documents do not become informal because they are signed after dinner.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: If the defence wishes to separate courtesy from commitment, it must explain the signature.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Dear Sirs, we act for the claimant resort in relation to the unpaid partnership obligations accepted by Mr. Swine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Our client reserves all rights arising from the signed cooperation and hospitality package.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The amount outstanding reflects services rendered, promotional preparation, and agreed exclusivity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Your client's silence following the invoice has caused our client additional concern.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Please confirm whether Pig & Swine accepts liability or proposes a timetable for payment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: In default of satisfactory response, our client will pursue arbitration without further delay.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: For the avoidance of doubt, Mr. Swine was treated throughout as a representative of Pig & Swine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The resort relied on the representations made during the signing event.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The suggestion that the arrangement was merely ceremonial is rejected.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: We remain open to commercial settlement, provided it is prompt, documented, and realistic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada

text: Good afternoon. I appear for the claimant resort.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
context: Mr. Yamada arrives / meets Pig & Swine
text: Dr. A. Cula, I understand you act for Pig & Swine and Mr. Swine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
context: Mr. Yamada arrives / meets Pig & Swine
text: Mr. Swine, it is good to see you again under circumstances involving fewer mountains and more documents.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
context: Mr. Yamada arrives / meets Pig & Swine
text: The resort remembers your visit with considerable warmth and increasing concern.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
context: Mr. Yamada arrives / meets Pig & Swine
text: I hope we can avoid unnecessary theatrics. The invoice already contains enough numbers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
context: Mr. Yamada arrives / meets Pig & Swine
text: This matter is commercially straightforward, even if your client's recollection is scenic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
context: Mr. Yamada arrives / meets Pig & Swine
text: Our case will focus on signature, outward authority, services rendered, and reliance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
context: Mr. Yamada arrives / meets Pig & Swine
text: I do not doubt Mr. Swine's charm. I doubt its legal usefulness.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
context: Mr. Yamada arrives / meets Pig & Swine
text: If the defence intends to rely on confusion, it should explain why confusion waited until payment was requested.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
context: Mr. Yamada arrives / meets Pig & Swine
text: The resort is not seeking punishment. It is seeking the value of what it provided.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Mr. Swine, you were quite clear at the resort when clarity brought benefits.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Your memory appears strongest where it reduces your obligations.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: You were introduced as a founder and senior representative. The resort was entitled to take that seriously.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: You accepted the event programme, promotional materials, and hospitality without protest.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The signing was photographed because both sides presented it as significant.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: If you believed the document was meaningless, your behaviour did not communicate that belief.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: You now describe the evening as ceremonial. At the time, you described it as a splendid opportunity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The resort did not invent reliance after the invoice. It acted on the understanding created there.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: I accept that you were charming. That is part of the problem.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: A smile may not bind a firm. A signature under commercial documents may.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Dr. A. Cula, your task is difficult because your client left you a charming factual landscape and very few guardrails.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: You may challenge scope. You may challenge quantum. You cannot pretend nothing happened.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The tribunal will not be assisted by converting every uncertainty into non-liability.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Your better argument is narrow. I suspect you know that.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: If you overstate the translation point, you will make my case easier.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The signature remains inconvenient to the defence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The resort's reliance was commercially reasonable on the facts available at the time.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: I respect careful advocacy. I will resist creative amnesia.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Your client may be foolish without being fraudulent. Foolishness still has consequences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Let us keep the case where it belongs: documents, authority, scope, and value.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Mr. Pig, your financial alarm is noted but not legally decisive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The claimant's invoice is not reduced by the respondent's surprise at its existence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: If Pig & Swine's internal finances are fragile, that does not answer whether the obligation was created.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Panic is not a counterclaim.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: I appreciate that the sum is uncomfortable. Commercial obligations often are.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Your maritime metaphors may be sincere, but they are not proof of non-liability.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The resort did not board your ship, Mr. Pig. It invoiced your firm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: If your office wished to limit Mr. Swine, it should have done so before he reached the signature table.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: I am not indifferent to the firm's position. I am instructed to recover what is owed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Please breathe. The tribunal will prefer it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Mr. Murrow, I suspect you and I agree on the importance of structure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The claimant's structure is simple: representation, signature, reliance, performance, invoice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: If you divide the defence into translation, authority, and quantum, we shall answer each.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: I respect your caution. It is unfortunate Mr. Swine did not borrow it earlier.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The tribunal will benefit from narrowing the issues.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The resort does not need theatrical certainty. It needs commercial reasonableness.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: If the defence identifies specific unsupported charges, we can address them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: If the defence denies the entire relationship, we will resist strongly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The signed document remains the central object.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: I am pleased the case may be argued like a case.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Crab's attention to line items is impressive and occasionally hostile.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: An invoice may contain categories unfamiliar to a small firm without becoming fictional.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Hospitality packages often bundle services.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Promotional preparation may occur even if the beneficiary later dislikes the bill.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada

speaker: Mr. Yamada

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The pudding line has attracted disproportionate attention.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada

speaker: Mr. Yamada

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: If the defence proves specific errors, quantum can be adjusted without collapsing liability.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Mr. Whimsy, the tribunal is unlikely to treat pudding as a constitutional category.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Your metaphor is colourful. The invoice is itemised.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The resort did not trap Mr. Swine in a snow-globe of obligation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada

notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: Commercial reliance is not tyranny because the claimant uses polite stationery.

speaker: Mr. Yamada

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: I appreciate your concern for fairness. I suggest you also read the signature page.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: A rights-based flourish cannot dissolve a hospitality package.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
text: The claimant is not an empire of pudding. It is a resort with unpaid invoices.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Yamada
